content.full_text:
City of Alameda California

JUL 0 3 1991

July 3, 1991

Ms. Lynne Martin
Legal Documents Examiner
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Martin:

Attached please find a copy of a letter you sent me dated June
14. I filed the additional information that you requested with
the Alameda City Clerk. The clerk informs me that the
information was indeed sent to the FPPC. If you have still not
received it, will you please call me. I work in Sacramento,
and can be reached at 916/327-4040.

Second, will you or someone in your office please be good
enough to help me answer a few questions:

1. I recently sent in a form 502 to open a new committee. The
FPPC (Alice Hughes) sent be back a note requesting I fill-
out a form 501, as well. As you know the 501 form is for
intention of candidacy. Well, I just completed a re-
election campaign (ID 903276) and want to open a "Friends
of" committee. Thus, I really do not have any intention
of running for anything else at the present time. How
should I handle this?

2. My "friends" committee held a fund raiser a few weeks ago on
June 20. Do I need to file a report at the end of July, or
do I wait until year end?

Any advice you might be able to offer on the above questions
would be much appreciated. I want to be in compliance with all
the rules and procedures and this is getting very complicated.
Again, I can be reached at 916/327-4040.

Sincerely,

Joe Camicia
Councilmember
Joseph Camicia, Councilmember

Office of the City Council, Room 315

City Hall
Santa Clara Avenue at Oak Street · 94501
415.748 4527 office, 415.748 4506 message

sections.question:
1. I recently sent in a form 502 to open a new committee. The FPPC (Alice Hughes) sent be back a note requesting I fill-out a form 501, as well. As you know the 501 form is for intention of candidacy. Well, I just completed a re-election campaign (ID 903275) and want to open a "Friends of" committee. Thus, I really do not have any intention of running for anything else at the present time. How should I handle this?

2. My "friends" committee held a fund raiser a few weeks ago on June 20. Do I need to file a report at the end of July, or do I wait until year end?
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks how to handle opening a "Friends of" committee after completing a re-election campaign when the FPPC requested a Form 501 (intention of candidacy), and whether the committee needs to file a report at the end of July following a June 20 fundraiser.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/letter from a requester to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are marked, but the letter contains two numbered questions embedded in the body text. Document appears to be a scanned image with some OCR errors (e.g., 'Jur : - il', 'i,iarLirr', '915' vs '916'). No FPPC advice or conclusion provided in this document.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from Councilmember Joseph Camicia of Alameda to the FPPC dated July 3, 1991, inquiring about filing requirements for a new "Friends of" committee and reporting deadlines following a fundraiser. The letter also references missing documents that were supposedly sent to the FPPC.
